note.text: Longstanding severe persistent asthma, multiple prior exacerbations.
Comorbid GERD, contributes to nocturnal cough.

coding.display: budesonide 0.16 MG/ACTUAT / formoterol 0.0045 MG/ACTUAT Inhalant Powder [Symbicort]

dosageInstruction.text: Inhale 2 puffs by mouth twice daily
Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime
Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 4-6 hours as needed

coding.display: omeprazole 20 MG Delayed Release Oral Capsule

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 capsule by mouth once daily before breakfast

resource.conclusion: Moderate-severe airflow obstruction. FEV1 2.2L (67% predicted), FEV1/FVC 67%. ACT 16 - poorly controlled. Elevated eosinophils and IgE support allergic phenotype.

text.div: Reports frequent rescue inhaler use, near-daily symptoms, occasional nocturnal awakening. One urgent-care visit in the past year (not formally documented here). Reflux symptoms most nights.
VS: BP 118/74, SpO2 95% RA. Lungs with diffuse expiratory wheeze. Spirometry: FEV1 2.2L, FVC 3.3L, FEV1/FVC 67%. PEF 250 L/min. ACT score 16. Labs: eosinophils 420/uL, IgE 260 IU/mL (both elevated).
Severe persistent asthma, poorly controlled, allergic phenotype. Comorbid GERD likely contributing to nocturnal symptoms.
Continue Symbicort and montelukast, start omeprazole for GERD. Discussed biologic therapy (omalizumab) if control doesn't improve. Follow-up in 6 months.

resource.conclusion: Essentially unchanged from prior. FEV1 2.25L, FEV1/FVC 68%. ACT 17 - still poorly controlled despite current regimen.

text.div: Continued near-daily symptoms, rescue inhaler 4-5x/week. Reflux improved somewhat on omeprazole.
VS: BP 120/76, SpO2 95% RA. Lungs with mild expiratory wheeze. Spirometry: FEV1 2.25L, FVC 3.3L, FEV1/FVC 68%. PEF 255 L/min. ACT score 17.
Severe persistent asthma, still poorly controlled on ICS/LABA + montelukast.
Continue current regimen. If control doesn't improve, plan to initiate omalizumab at next visit. Follow-up in 4 months.

resource.conclusion: Acute severe asthma exacerbation, SpO2 90% on presentation, FEV1 1.7L. Treated with nebulized bronchodilators and systemic corticosteroids, improved and discharged home with close follow-up arranged.

text.div: Presented with 2 days of worsening wheeze, chest tightness, and rescue inhaler not providing relief. Upper respiratory infection preceded symptoms.
SpO2 90% on room air on arrival, improved to 96% after treatment. Diffuse wheeze on exam. FEV1 1.7L. Treated with nebulized albuterol/ipratropium and IV corticosteroids.
Acute severe asthma exacerbation, likely viral trigger, on background of severe persistent asthma with suboptimal baseline control.
Discharge on prednisone burst, continue home regimen, close follow-up in 3 days. Reconsider step-up therapy (biologic) given recurrent poor control.

resource.conclusion: Post-exacerbation follow-up. FEV1 2.0L, recovering toward baseline. ACT 15 - poorly controlled. Recurrent exacerbations despite ICS/LABA + LTRA; starting omalizumab today.

text.div: Feeling better since the ED visit, completed prednisone burst. Still using rescue inhaler daily.
SpO2 94% RA. Lungs with trace wheeze, improved from ED presentation. FEV1 2.0L. ACT score 15.
Severe persistent asthma with recurrent exacerbations despite triple therapy (ICS/LABA + LTRA).
Initiate omalizumab 150mg SC every 4 weeks. Continue current inhalers. Follow-up in 6-8 weeks to assess biologic response.

resource.conclusion: Severe acute exacerbation despite omalizumab therapy, SpO2 89% on presentation, FEV1 1.6L. Admitted overnight for observation and nebulized treatment, discharged next morning. Outpatient follow-up needed to reassess biologic response and adherence.

text.div: Presented with acute shortness of breath and wheeze after running out of controller inhaler for approximately one week. Reports missing several omalizumab doses due to scheduling issues.
SpO2 89% on room air on arrival, improved to 95% overnight. Diffuse wheeze. FEV1 1.6L. Treated with nebulized bronchodilators, IV corticosteroids, and observation overnight.
Acute severe asthma exacerbation in the setting of a gap in controller therapy and missed omalizumab doses.
Discharge on prednisone taper, resume home inhalers. Recommend close outpatient follow-up within 1-2 weeks to reassess control, adherence, and omalizumab dosing schedule.
